title: Mouthwatering Crunchy Indian Lentil Vada
description: A briny and crunchy Indian dish featuring ground nut oil and lentils, perfect for a snack or side dish. This recipe is also anti-inflammatory friendly.
instructions: Grind the soaked and drained lentils into a coarse paste using a food processor or blender.
Heat the ground nut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and coriander seeds to the oil and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the chopped onion, grated ginger, minced garlic, and chopped green chili to the oil and saute until the onion is translucent.
Add the lentil paste to the skillet and stir well to combine with the onion mixture.
Add the salt and water to the lentil mixture and stir well to combine.
Using your hands, shape the lentil mixture into small patties or vadas.
Add the vadas to the hot oil and fry until they are golden brown and crispy on both sides.
Remove the vadas from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the vadas hot with your favorite chutney or raita.
